# Platform Intelligence — Founder Demo (asset format)

Practitioner-grade reference for the **founder-led product demo video** — the launch-day asset that
shows the product working in the founder's own voice. Consumed by `brief-shortform`, `produce-video`,
`brief-app-preview`, and the launch chains (it's the Product Hunt gallery video, the landing hero
demo, the launch-tweet clip). The bar is *credibility + clarity*, not production gloss — a founder who
clearly shows the thing working out-converts a slick agency reel.

When `last_verified` exceeds 90 days, re-verify the retention/structure norms.

---

## 1. Demo Angles (Hook Taxonomy, reframed)

### Angle 1 — Problem-first (the "I built this because…")

- **Definition:** Open on the specific pain the founder personally hit, then cut straight to the
  product solving it. The story earns the demo.
- **Identifying signal:** First 3s name a real, felt problem; founder on camera or voiceover; no logo-splash intro.
- **Best for:** founder mode, products with a genuine origin; PH gallery, landing hero.

### Angle 2 — Cold-open demo (show the magic in second one)

- **Definition:** Open ON the core action working — the satisfying result first, the explanation
  after. "Here's what it does" before "here's why."
- **Identifying signal:** Frame 1 is the product mid-action/result; narration catches up.
- **Best for:** visually-satisfying products, short social cuts, scroll-stopping.

### Angle 3 — Before/after (the transformation)

- **Definition:** The painful old way, then the product's new way, side by side or sequential.
- **Identifying signal:** A clear contrast the viewer feels; the delta is the hook.
- **Best for:** tools that replace a tedious workflow.

---

## 2. Format Constraints

| Constraint | Value | reference |
|---|---|---|
| Length | 30–90s for launch (≤60s ideal for social/PH gallery); a 2–4 min deep version optional | demo practice |
| Hook | the problem/result in the **first 3 seconds** | retention practice |
| Show the real product | screen-record/real UI — NOT slides or a mockup deck | demo practice |
| One core action | demo the single highest-value action, end to end | demo practice |
| Captions | burned-in (most launch views are sound-off) | social practice |
| Aspect | 16:9 for landing/PH, 9:16 cut for social/Reels/Shorts | platform packs |
| Audio | clear founder voice; quiet room > fancy mic with echo | production practice |
| Resolution | 1080p min; crisp UI text (don't downscale the screen-record) | production practice |
| CTA | one, at the end (and pinned/linked outside the video) | conversion practice |

---

## 4. Anti-Patterns

| Pattern | Penalty | Detection | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Slides/mockups instead of the real product | reads as vaporware; kills trust | no actual UI shown working | demo practice |
| Slow logo-splash intro | viewers drop before the value | first 3s are branding, not the hook | retention practice |
| Feature firehose | viewer lost; no single takeaway | >1 core action demoed; laundry list | demo practice |
| Over-produced / inauthentic | reads as an ad, not a founder | agency gloss, scripted-stiff VO | founder-content practice |
| No captions | half the audience watches muted, gets nothing | sound-dependent demo | social practice |
| Buried/absent CTA | no next step | demo ends with no ask | conversion practice |
| Tiny/illegible UI text | the demo can't be followed | downscaled screen-record | production practice |

---

## 5. Playbook / Tactical Sequence

**Highest-leverage tactic:** show the ONE core action *actually working* in the real product within
the first 10 seconds, in the founder's own voice — credibility + clarity beat polish.

| # | Step | Concrete action | Success marker |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Pick the one action | the single highest-value thing the product does | a stranger could name the value after watching |
| 2 | Choose the angle | problem-first / cold-open / before-after (§1) | hook lands in 3s |
| 3 | Script the spine (not every word) | hook → problem → demo the action → outcome → CTA; bullet beats, founder improvises | sounds human, not read |
| 4 | Screen-record the real UI | clean account, legible text, the action end-to-end, no dead air | the product visibly works |
| 5 | Voice it | founder VO/on-camera; quiet room; one clean take or light cuts | clear, credible, unstiff |
| 6 | Caption + cut | burn captions; cut to ≤60s for social, keep a 16:9 for PH/landing | sound-off legible; right lengths |
| 7 | End on the action + CTA | one CTA; outcome shown; link pinned outside the video | clear next step |
| 8 | Measure | which cut/angle converted → `/measure-results` into the channel pack | next demo compounds |

---

## 7. CTA / Conversion Norms

| Placement | When it works | When it fails | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| End-card CTA (after the outcome) | viewer saw the value first | front-loaded ask | conversion practice |
| Pinned link / first comment (PH, social) | native to the surface | relying on an in-video clickable | platform packs |
| "Try it / waitlist" verbal + on-screen | clear, single ask | multiple competing CTAs | conversion practice |

---

## 8. Open Questions / Known Unknowns

- Optimal length varies by surface + audience; ≤60s is a strong default, not a universal rule — A/B per channel.
- The polish-vs-authenticity tradeoff has no fixed point; for early-stage founder content authenticity usually wins, but high-ticket B2B may want more polish.
